description: Replication, RPCs, multiplayer networking, HTTP requests, REST APIs, and online subsystems in UE5

entries.title: Property Not Replicating / Client Not Seeing Changes
entries.description: A UPROPERTY marked as Replicated doesn't sync from server to clients. The server value changes but clients see the old value.
entries.rootCause: The property is marked with UPROPERTY(Replicated) but GetLifetimeReplicatedProps() doesn't include the DOREPLIFETIME macro for it. Both are required. Also, only the server can change replicated properties - client changes are overwritten.
solution: Add UPROPERTY(Replicated) or UPROPERTY(ReplicatedUsing=OnRep_FunctionName) to the property
Implement GetLifetimeReplicatedProps() and add DOREPLIFETIME(ClassName, PropertyName)
Ensure the owning Actor has bReplicates = true (set in constructor)
Only modify replicated properties on the server (HasAuthority() == true)
For conditional replication, use DOREPLIFETIME_CONDITION with the appropriate condition
Check that the Actor is relevant to the client (net relevancy)

entries.title: RPC Not Executing / Server/Client Function Not Called
entries.description: A UFUNCTION marked as Server, Client, or NetMulticast RPC doesn't execute on the target machine.
entries.rootCause: Server RPCs must be called from the owning client's connection. Client RPCs must be called from the server on an actor owned by that client. The actor must also be replicated (bReplicates = true). Calling a Server RPC on an actor you don't own silently fails.
solution: Server RPC: call from the owning client. The PlayerController owns the RPC channel
Client RPC: call from the server on the player's owned actor (PlayerController or its Pawn)
NetMulticast: call from the server - it executes on server AND all clients
Ensure the actor has bReplicates = true
Server RPCs need _Implementation suffix on the actual function body
Add Reliable or Unreliable: Reliable guarantees delivery, Unreliable may drop under load
Verify ownership chain: Pawn is owned by PlayerController which is owned by the connection

entries.title: Client Prediction / Rubber Banding Issues
entries.description: Player characters teleport back, jitter, or 'rubber band' during multiplayer gameplay. Movement feels laggy or corrects visually.
entries.rootCause: The Character Movement Component uses client-side prediction. When the server's simulation disagrees with the client's prediction (due to latency, packet loss, or server corrections), the client is snapped to the server's position.
solution: Tune CharacterMovementComponent network settings: NetworkSmoothingMode, NetworkMaxSmoothUpdateDistance
Increase server tick rate for more frequent updates
Enable client-side prediction smoothing with bSmoothClientPosition
For custom movement: implement FCharacterMovementComponent prediction correctly (SavedMoves)
Reduce server correction threshold: NetworkSimulatedSmoothLocationTime / RotationTime
For non-character actors, implement your own interpolation/extrapolation
Test with simulated latency: use Net PktLag, Net PktLoss console commands

entries.title: HTTP Request Fails / REST API Integration Issues
entries.description: FHttpRequest to external APIs fails, returns errors, or doesn't fire the callback.
entries.rootCause: Common issues: the HTTP module isn't included in Build.cs, SSL certificate verification fails for self-signed certs, the response callback captures a destroyed object, or the request is garbage collected before completion.
solution: Add 'HTTP' and 'Json' to your Build.cs PublicDependencyModuleNames
Store the TSharedRef<IHttpRequest> as a member variable so it's not garbage collected
For SSL errors, set the request to ignore SSL with Request->SetVerb(TEXT('GET')) and proper headers
Check that the URL is accessible from the game's network (firewall, CORS doesn't apply to game clients)
Parse JSON responses with FJsonSerializer::Deserialize()
Bind the callback to a UObject member function (not a lambda capturing 'this') to avoid dangling references

entries.title: Actor Not Spawning on Clients / Replicated Actor Missing
entries.description: An actor spawned on the server doesn't appear on clients even though it should be replicated.
entries.rootCause: The actor doesn't have bReplicates=true, or it's outside the client's net relevancy distance. Actors spawned on clients (not server) are local-only. The actor class may also not be loaded on the client.
solution: Set bReplicates = true in the actor's constructor
Spawn actors ONLY on the server - they will automatically replicate to clients
Check net relevancy: increase NetCullDistanceSquared for actors that should be visible at long range
Set bAlwaysRelevant = true for actors that must exist on all clients regardless of distance
Ensure the actor class and its assets are available on the client (cooked content)
For dynamic spawn, ensure the server calls SpawnActor, not the client

entries.title: Online Subsystem / Session Management Issues
entries.description: The Online Subsystem (Steam, EOS, etc.) fails to initialize, create sessions, or find sessions.
entries.rootCause: The online subsystem plugin isn't enabled or configured correctly in DefaultEngine.ini. Steam requires the Steamworks SDK and a valid AppID. EOS requires proper product/sandbox/deployment IDs.
solution: Enable the appropriate online subsystem plugin (OnlineSubsystemSteam, OnlineSubsystemEOS, etc.)
Configure DefaultEngine.ini with the correct subsystem settings
For Steam: create a steam_appid.txt with your AppID (or 480 for testing) in the Binaries folder
For EOS: set ProductId, SandboxId, DeploymentId, ClientId, ClientSecret in DefaultEngine.ini
Use IOnlineSubsystem::Get() to verify the subsystem is available before using it
Test with OnlineSubsystemNull for local testing without a backend

entries.title: Network Authority / Role Confusion
entries.description: Code runs on the wrong machine (server vs client) or authority checks don't work as expected, leading to desync or duplicate logic.
entries.rootCause: Confusion between network roles: Authority (server), Autonomous Proxy (owning client), Simulated Proxy (other clients). HasAuthority() is true on the server (or for non-replicated actors). GetLocalRole()/GetRemoteRole() return the actor's role on the current machine.
solution: Use HasAuthority() to check if you're on the server for this actor
Use IsLocallyControlled() to check if this is the owning player's machine
Server: HasAuthority()=true, runs game logic, spawns actors, applies damage
Owning client (Autonomous Proxy): sends input to server via RPCs, runs prediction
Other clients (Simulated Proxy): receive replicated state, run cosmetic effects only
Use switch on GetLocalRole() for role-specific behavior

entries.title: WebSocket Connection Issues
entries.description: WebSocket connections fail to establish, disconnect unexpectedly, or the WebSocket module isn't available.
entries.rootCause: The WebSockets module isn't added to Build.cs, the connection URL format is wrong, or the server requires specific subprotocols/headers that aren't set.
solution: Add 'WebSockets' to your Build.cs PublicDependencyModuleNames
Use the correct URL scheme: ws:// for unencrypted, wss:// for encrypted
Set required headers and protocols before connecting
Implement OnConnected, OnConnectionError, OnClosed, OnMessage delegates
Handle reconnection logic - WebSockets don't auto-reconnect
Keep a reference to IWebSocket as a member variable to prevent GC

entries.title: Network Bandwidth / Too Much Replication Traffic
entries.description: Multiplayer game uses too much bandwidth, causing lag, dropped packets, or server performance issues.
entries.rootCause: Too many actors replicating, properties updating too frequently, large RPCs, or not using net relevancy properly. Every replicated property change generates network traffic.
solution: Use DOREPLIFETIME_CONDITION to conditionally replicate (COND_OwnerOnly, COND_SkipOwner, COND_InitialOnly)
Reduce NetUpdateFrequency on actors that don't need frequent updates
Set proper net relevancy distances - distant actors shouldn't replicate
Use Unreliable RPCs for non-critical updates (cosmetic effects, audio cues)
Quantize replicated values: use uint8 for health (0-255) instead of float
Use the Network Profiler tool: 'stat net' and Unreal Insights networking track
Batch RPC calls instead of sending many small RPCs
